original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: United Arab Emirates Ministry of Health and Prevention
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Attestation of Medical Leaves and Reports
original_registry_entry.version: MOHAP service code 110-10-019-000; webpage last updated 10 July 2026
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United Arab Emirates federal service, with competent-emirate authority qualifications
original_registry_entry.population: Patients of all ages submitting a medical leave, companion leave, or medical report for attestation.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Administrative attestation of documents issued by licensed UAE health facilities or appropriately certified foreign institutions; not a clinical assessment standard.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for document type, licensed-facility, attestation, duration-band and medical-committee requirements. It does not define clinical incapacity, fitness, diagnosis disclosure, assessment content, dates, duration, backdating, or school-note requirements.

exact_sections.heading: About this service; required documents; target audience
exact_sections.locator: official service page sections “About this service”, “Required documents”, and “Target audience”
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording that an issued sick-leave certificate or related medical report is being submitted for employer-facing attestation and distinguishes sick leave, companion leave and medical reports without establishing clinical incapacity.

exact_sections.heading: Conditions and requirements
exact_sections.locator: official service page section “Conditions and requirements”
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports licensed-facility, competent-authority, issuance-to-attestation interval, duration-band and medical-committee approval context; it does not authorize automatic certification, backdating, duration selection, or diagnosis disclosure.

title: Attestation of Medical Leaves and Reports
publisher: United Arab Emirates Ministry of Health and Prevention
jurisdiction: United Arab Emirates federal service, with competent-emirate authority qualifications
population: Patients of all ages submitting a medical leave, companion leave, or medical report for attestation.
intended_setting: Administrative attestation of documents issued by licensed UAE health facilities or appropriately certified foreign institutions; not a clinical assessment standard.
